snippet.title: Noor | Atif Aslam & Sami Yusuf
snippet.description:
#atifaslam #samiyusuf #sufimusic #aadeez 
On November 7, 2025, a landmark moment in live music took place at the Etihad Arena in Abu Dhabi as Sami Yusuf and Atif Aslam shared a stage for the first time in their careers.

The performance marked the debut live collaboration between two of the most influential musicians of their generation. Their presentation of ‘Noor’ was a standout moment that brought together their distinct musical identities in a powerful and seamless exchange.

Joining was an ensemble of master musicians, providing rich acoustic textures from the resonant strings of the tar and santur to the expressive tones of the kamancheh that wove seamlessly with sitar and tablas to create a deeply immersive sonic landscape.The audience responded with palpable intensity, enveloped in the sound with focus and reverence, underscoring the significance of the occasion.

The brilliance of this composition lies precisely in its layering of classical Arabic praise imagery with Quranic metaphor, its recitation style evoking Turkic ceremony, its qawwali structure, and its contemporary vocal arrangement. Add to this the soaring vocals of Atif Aslam and Sami Yusuf, and the listeners know they are in front of a masterpiece.

Credits
Composer and Musical Director: Sami Yusuf
Vocalists: Atif Aslam and Sami Yusuf

Arabic: Traditional
Urdu: Ilma Qureshi

Mixed by Vishnu Rajan
Mastered by Gavin Lurssen
Video Editing & Postproduction by Omar Al Balushi @ Andante Studios

Performed by the Sami Yusuf Ensemble & Atif Aslam

Recorded live at the Etihad Arena in Abu Dhabi

©℗ 2026 Andante Records. All rights reserved.

Naat Mere Likh Wasiyat Khaghaz Te

Arif Lohar was born in 1966 in Lala Musa in Gujrat District, Punjab, Pakistan. His father was Alam Lohar, who belonged to the village of Achh in nearby Kharian Tehsil, and was a prominent folk singer.[2] Arif Lohar belongs to the Mughal Lohar Family. Arif Lohar started singing with his father and his eldest brother, Arshad Mahmood Lohar and younger brother Tariq Lohar at an early age.

He has 7 brothers named Arshad Mahmood Lohar, Khalid Mahmood Lohar, Tariq Lohar, Irfan Mahmood Lohar, Imran Mahmood Lohar, Basharat Lohar, Faisal Lohar who all live in the UK. Arif Lohar is the only one of Alam Lohar's 8 sons who lives in Pakistan along with his family.

Arif Lohar briefly went into acting in Punjabi movies before returning to his music career at the age of 22.

snippet.title: Aayat Arif || Ramadan Ki Kya Baat Hai || Ramadan Special 2026 || Official video
snippet.description:
#aayatarif  #ramadan  #ramadankikyabaathai

🌙 Assalam-O-Alaikum Wa Rahmatullahi Wa Barakatuh! 🌙

RAMADAN MUBARAK TO YOU AND YOUR FAMILY! ✨

A very special hello to everyone watching! As the moon is sighted and the holy month begins, our hearts fill with peace. To celebrate this blessing, I am so happy to present my brand new Kalam, "Ramdan Ki Kya Baat Hai."

This month isn't just about staying hungry or thirsty; it’s about feeling the love of Allah. It’s about sharing, caring, and fixing our hearts. This little nasheed is my humble attempt to capture that beautiful feeling—the peace of Sehri, the blessing of Iftar, and the sweetness of Taraweeh.

🎧 In this soulful voice of Aayat Arif, listen to what makes Ramadan so special! 🎧

snippet.title: Sun Charkhe Di Mithi Mithi Ghook (Lyric Video) | Ustad Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan Songs |Qawwali | #NFAK
snippet.description:
🎵 Sun Charkhe Di Mithi Mithi Ghook — A timeless Punjabi folk song that beautifully captures the sweetness of love, longing, and village life. Its soulful lyrics and melodious rhythm bring out the warmth of true emotions and the charm of Punjabi culture. Let this classic tune take you back to the roots of love and simplicity. 🌾💖

🎧 A melody filled with love, nostalgia, and soulful emotions.

#SunCharkheDiMithiMithiGhook #folkmusic #culturalmelody #soulfulmusic 

Subscribe for more Punjabi classics, folk songs, and soulful renditions. 🌹

Listen Now On -
Spotify : https://open.spotify.com/track/24kXg06yoe2H1Vy74m5QOF?si=407e5da9ab554a3d
Apple Music : https://music.apple.com/us/song/sun-charkhe-di-mithi-mithi-ghook/910383709
YouTube Music : https://music.youtube.com/watch?v=ogTjOq0GKiM&si=s3ylugNRzjAPH_zL

Watch More: https://www.youtube.com/@OrientalStarAgencies/playlists

Credits -
Vocalist - Ustad Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
Composer - Ustad Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
Lyricist - Bari Nizami

Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan (13 October 1948 – 16 August 1997) was a Pakistani musician, primarily a singer of Qawwali, the devotional music of the Sufis. Widely considered one of the greatest voices ever recorded, he possessed an extraordinary range of vocal abilities and could perform at a high level of intensity for several hours. Extending the 600-year old Qawwali tradition of his family, Khan is widely credited with introducing Qawwali music to international audiences. He is popularly known as "Shahenshah-e-Qawwali", meaning "The King of Qawwali". Born in Faisalabad, Khan had his first public performance at the age of 16, at his father's chelum. He became the head of the family qawwali party in 1971. He was signed by Oriental Star Agencies, Birmingham, England. He engaged in collaborations and experiments with Western artists, becoming a well-known world music artist. He toured extensively, performing in over 40 countries.


(c) Oriental Star Agencies
